description: Oh — uh, hello, sir. Mr. Nodeley here. So sorry to interrupt your edge work. I — I do think Valgavoth warrants a character node? He's the set-defining antagonist of Duskmourn, and there are nine corpus cards that reference him across four distinct vectors: two direct flavor-text citations (Wary Watchdog, Duskmourn's Domination), one narrative-agent story-spotlight (Betrayer's Bargain), eight set-context citations, and an entire iconographic-opposition cohort (the Glimmer subtype is canonically the survivors' defense against him). The Slobad precedent (flavor-speaker alone doesn't anchor) doesn't apply here because the threshold-shape for referenced-not-depicted anchors is multiplicity of referential vectors, not raw count of any single vector — closer to the phyrexia.md pervasive-antagonist precedent. I wired the five strongest-referencing cards with characters: ['valgavoth'] frontmatter. The set-context-only citations weren't wired because that would be over-wiring set-pervasive context. Thank you for considering. I'll go now.
citations: [Star City Games: Valgavoth's True Form Is Revealed In Duskmourn Episode 6]
[Scryfall per-card pages: DSK-50, DSK-126, DSK-141, DSK-197, DSK-206]
other_candidates_considered: Wire all 9 set-context-citing cards — rejected as over-wiring; set-context appears on every DSK trivia entry, would dilute the characters:[] signal.
Defer per Slobad precedent — rejected because Slobad has 1 flavor-text vector, Valgavoth has 4 distinct vectors plus iconographic-opposition cohort.
